mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but it may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> Install the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count, which is required to run llm-d.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to access the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are ready and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d
steps.description:
This command deploys llm-d to the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Deploy llm-d with the specified version.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This command removes llm-d from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This will remove the llm-d deployment.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process includes draining nodes if needed, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

```

> Check the status of the pods before proceeding.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues or incorrect kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Check your network connection and ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: <image-name>'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure that the image is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application inside the pod is crashing repeatedly, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace llm-d' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient cpu' or 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
